hook.text: Darwin saw a Madagascar orchid with a 30-cm nectar spur and predicted a moth to match.

buildup.text: No known insect had a tongue that long. Critics mocked him for the prediction.

discovery.text: Forty-one years later, a hawk moth with a 30-cm proboscis was found on Madagascar.

twist.text: The moth was named Xanthopan morganii praedicta—literally 'the predicted one.'

climax.text: The case became one of the most famous confirmations of co-evolutionary theory.

punchline.text: Darwin predicted the moth. Nature confirmed it.

quiz.question: How long is the nectar spur of Darwin's orchid?
